description: Fully remove Owner Scope from Pibo's active product model, schemas, APIs, CLI/TUI, automation, workflows, tests, and current docs. Auth remains only an access gate; legacy data is handled through a safe final cutover migration without using shared:app as a replacement owner.

userStories.title: Establish final owner-scope removal baseline and progress tracking
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want an implementation baseline and progress record so that the Ralph loop can remove Owner Scope across the app without losing traceability.
acceptanceCriteria: Create or update IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md with branch, worktree, Docker worker, PRD paths, current status, and story progress
Link the plan, PRD, inventory summary, and raw inventory files

Record baseline rg counts for ownerScope, owner_scope, OwnerScope, owner-scope, shared:app, principalId, principal_id, room_members, listOwned, getOwned, requireOwned, personal target, active owner, and --owner-scope
Record read-only SQLite schema/column inventory for reachable affected stores without mutating data
Run baseline npm run typecheck and npm run build, and record results
Do not change runtime behavior in this story
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add strict owner-scope search gates with temporary allowlist
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want automated gates for Owner Scope vocabulary so that new work cannot reintroduce product ownership artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a test or script that fails on active product matches for ownerScope, owner_scope, OwnerScope, owner-scope, shared:app, principalId, principal_id, room_members, listOwned, getOwned, and requireOwned
Support a narrow temporary allowlist for the final migration module and historical docs only
Document how to run the gate and how to shrink the allowlist to zero after cutover
Add focused tests for the gate behavior
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Replace shared-app legacy owner helpers with neutral app context

acceptanceCriteria: Remove LEGACY_SHARED_APP_OWNER_SCOPE and getSharedAppLegacyOwnerScope from active runtime code
Keep a neutral app context representation that has no legacyOwnerScope or ownerScope field
Update imports and callers to stop requesting a replacement owner value
Focused tests cover neutral app context creation and absence of legacy owner fields
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Make web auth an access gate without product owner fields
userStories.description: As an allowed web user, I want login to grant app access without creating product ownership state so that two accounts enter the same app context.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove ownerScope from PiboWebSession and related web auth/session types
Keep unauthenticated 401 behavior where auth is required
Ensure authenticated handlers receive auth identity only for access/display/audit, not visibility or write location
Add tests proving two allowed auth identities resolve to the same app context and no owner field
Focused web auth tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create final owner-scope cutover migration inspect and dry-run
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a final migration command that reports old Owner Scope data without mutating it so that cutover can be reviewed safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Create an operator-only final cutover migration module/command with inspect and dry-run modes
Inspect reports affected files, tables, columns, indexes, row counts, legacy values, and conflict groups without secrets
Dry-run reports planned table rebuilds, merge/rename decisions, and unresolved blockers without writes
Command refuses Production apply unless explicit operator flags and backup path are provided in later apply story
Migration inspect/dry-run fixture tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement final owner-scope cutover migration apply and rollback checks
userStories.description: As an operator, I want transactional apply behavior with backup verification and post-checks so that owner columns can be removed safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Apply mode requires a fresh verified backup path and refuses unresolved conflicts
Apply rebuilds affected SQLite tables without owner/principal columns, membership tables, or owner indexes
Apply is transactional per database file and idempotent on already-migrated schemas
Post-check runs row-count checks and PRAGMA quick_check and writes a migration report
Rollback instructions are generated or documented with the backup path
Migration apply/idempotency/rollback fixture tests pass
Typecheck passes
